title: Steven Spielberg's Top 5 Movies With Bill Simmons and Chris Ryan | The Big Picture (Ep. 57)
format: Chris Ryan and Sean Fennessy each build a Spielberg top five, then Bill Simmons drops in to argue with both of them.

films.title: Ready Player One
films.note: The new release prompting the conversation; Chris hasn't seen it going in.

films.title: The Post
films.note: Namechecked as the Best Picture nominee Spielberg made four months earlier.

films.title: Jaws
films.note: His pick for a perfect movie: 'drama, humor, scares, laughs, tension, humanity, great performances, great music.'

films.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark
films.note: The 'small adventure story' he says spawned a whole unraveling world of IP without meaning to.

films.title: Empire of the Sun
films.note: A personal pick: his dad's WWII childhood tracked Christian Bale's. Praises Tom Stoppard's script and Malkovich.

films.title: Saving Private Ryan
films.note: Calls the first 40 minutes the most virtuosic combat filmmaking ever shot, but says the back half runs baggy.

films.title: Jurassic Park
films.note: Praised for being lean, 'a three or four-hander' that still goes hard for a spectacle movie.

films.title: Jaws
films.note: The shared No. 1 with Chris; he watches it every Fourth of July.

films.title: Jurassic Park
films.note: Calls it the 'median point' of Spielberg's career: spectacle, childhood, and pure IP at once.

films.title: Close Encounters of the Third Kind
films.note: The child-of-divorce movie, he says, with Truffaut's performance and Dreyfuss anchoring what he calls the most 70s American film in Spielberg's filmography.

films.title: Schindler's List
films.note: Comes with the story of Billy Wilder wanting to make this himself before Spielberg won the rights.

films.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark
films.note: 'The best action hero star performance': Harrison Ford as 'Clark Kent and Superman at the same time.'

films.title: Minority Report
films.note: Chris's No. 6, 'shockingly cynical,' the darkest popcorn movie Spielberg made.

films.title: Amistad
films.note: Grouped with Munich and Empire of the Sun as one of the 'master manipulator' serious films.

films.title: War of the Worlds
films.note: Chris says it almost cracked his top five.

films.title: Hook
films.note: A soft spot for both: Dustin Hoffman's Captain Hook and the Julia Roberts tabloid chaos around the shoot.

films.title: The Adventures of Tintin
films.note: Filed under 'frivolous.'

films.title: The BFG
films.note: Called 'completely uninteresting and bad.'

films.title: The Terminal
films.note: Same verdict as The BFG: hard to watch.

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull
films.note: The one they'd rather forget, cast without Shia LaBeouf's arc paying off.

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
films.note: Where Spielberg met Kate Capshaw, on a timeline Bill later disputes in the bonus segment.

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
films.note: Chris's answer for where the franchise should have ended.

films.title: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial
films.note: Sean's lukewarm and knows it'll get him in trouble at home; Chris would rather rewatch Lincoln.

films.title: Munich
films.note: Sean's high on it; Chris calls it overrated but says the opening 35 minutes silence the room.

films.title: Catch Me If You Can
films.note: Chris's pick for best Spielberg performance: DiCaprio 'turned on to 100,' rare for him outside Django.

films.title: Lincoln
films.note: Daniel Day-Lewis gets the same 'take my money' treatment as DiCaprio in Catch Me If You Can.

films.title: Christopher Robin
films.note: Cited as basically a stealth Hook remake with Ewan McGregor.

segments.heading: Bonus: Bill Simmons on Spielberg
segments.note: the podfather's first Big Picture appearance

films.title: Jaws
films.note: Bill's pick for the best movie Spielberg ever made, and the first modern movie that still fully holds up.

films.title: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial
films.note: Bill's case for one of the five greatest movies ever, especially once he watched it with his own kids.

films.title: Jurassic Park
films.note: 'He made the best movie of anybody that decade out of all those young guys.'

films.title: Saving Private Ryan
films.note: On the first 20 minutes: 'absolutely one of the best 20-minute stretches ever.'

films.title: Close Encounters of the Third Kind
films.note: Bill's take: it hasn't aged as well as the others, and drags watching it now with his kids.

films.title: Minority Report
films.note: Bill's conflicted: 'a movie I'd never watch again,' though Chris disagrees.

films.title: Munich
films.note: Bill likes it 'a lot less than most people' and wishes a director like Michael Mann had taken the assassination-thriller angle.

films.title: Catch Me If You Can
films.note: Bill agrees it's a real swing, even among the Spielberg movies he doesn't fully buy.

Close Encounters of the Third Kind.title: Close Encounters of the Third Kind

Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Cry.title: Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull

Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom

Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade.title: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
